name: Spectral Freeze
description: This algorithm is an audio ‘freeze’ effect – it captures sound at a moment in time and sustains it indefinitely. The ‘spectral’ part of the name indicates that it works in the frequency domain – unlike simple loopers or granular effects, which work in the time domain, this algorithm captures the spectrum of a sound (using an FFT) and uses that to resynthesize audio. As well as completely freezing sound, the algorithm can actually play it back at any desired speed (i.e. it does time stretching) and pitch. There are a number (depending on the specification) of different ‘voices’ i.e. you can freeze different moments of audio and play them simultaneously.

specifications.name: History
specifications.description: The length of the history stored for each voice.

specifications.name: FFT size
specifications.description: The size of the FFT used internally.

parameters.name: Freeze toggle
parameters.description: Activates a new freeze when the parameter changes from ‘0’ to ‘1’.

parameters.name: Freeze gate
parameters.description: Activates a new freeze when the parameter changes from ‘0’ to ‘1’, and releases the freeze when the parameter changes from ‘1’ to ‘0’.

parameters.name: Freeze target
parameters.description: Chooses the voice for the next freeze, or ‘0’ for ‘Auto’.

parameters.name: Unfreeze all
parameters.description: When this parameter changes to ‘1’, all active freezes are released.

parameters.name: Rate
parameters.description: Sets the rate of movement. ‘0’ is stopped; ‘1000’ is ‘normal speed’ (as recorded). In between, the scale is highly non-linear to give more control at slower speeds.

parameters.name: Depth
parameters.description: Sets the maximum amount of movement.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Offsets the freeze position back in time.

parameters.name: Etherization
parameters.description: Drops out transient elements of the sound.

parameters.name: Live process
parameters.description: When this parameter changes to ‘1’, a new voice is started which uses live audio input instead of actually freezing. This is provided mainly as a means to use the etherization effect on live audio.

parameters.name: Coarse shift
parameters.description: Sets the coarse pitch shift amount for all voices. Added to the individual voice shifts.

parameters.name: Fine shift
parameters.description: Sets the fine pitch shift amount for all voices. Added to the individual voice shifts.

parameters.name: Freeze (per-voice)
parameters.description: Activates a new freeze on the corresponding voice when the parameter changes from ‘0’ to ‘1’, and releases the freeze when the parameter changes from ‘1’ to ‘0’.

parameters.name: Coarse shift (per-voice)
parameters.description: Sets the coarse pitch shift amount.

parameters.name: Fine shift (per-voice)
parameters.description: Sets the fine pitch shift amount.

parameters.name: Output (per-voice)
parameters.description: Sets the output bus for the voice.

parameters.name: Output mode (per-voice)
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

output_ports.name: Output
output_ports.description: Audio output from the spectral freeze voices.
